label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern cmp,vp,vpc,naive = L,W,W,_ : cmplog BLOCKS, value_profile + value_profile_cmplog RESOLVE, naive non-decisive. The only technique delta between the involved winner (vp/vpc) and loser (cmplog) is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback (both carry the same I2S substitution mutator). The shape's Layer-1 attribution is therefore VP CMP_MAP gradient; this test is the INDEPENDENT real-data verification (G2) of HOW that gradient advantage manifests.
round0_refuted: Round-0 bet the whole shape on ONE story: VP's gradient closes scalar value-DISTANCE at the gate operand (value_distance_reached -> vp_min_distance << cmp_min_distance, a residual Hamming/magnitude gap). The deterministic arbiter scored that against real seeds and NONE held. The scalar-distance-closure mechanism is REFUTED and is NOT re-proposed (a relaxed re-test is forbidden).
round1_outcome: Round-1: vp_enriches_gate_operand_bytes (operand_enrichment) CONFIRMED the 3 harfbuzz codepoint gates (6025/6037/6048) -> FROZEN. vp_admits_token_richer_assembly (joint_necessity) held for >=1 branch (KEEP).
round2_outcome: Round-2 added vp_drives_corpus_deeper_into_assembly (depth_reach, rule depth_lift>=2.0 AND winner_branch_taken AND NOT loser_branch_taken). It CONFIRMED >=2 branches (now FROZEN) but scored rule_not_met for libpng_3833, libxml2_6792/6800/7069_h0/7138_h1, sqlite3_14493. Diagnosis: (a) the rule referenced winner_branch_taken / loser_branch_taken, which the depth_reach tool does NOT emit -- it emits winner_side_depth / loser_side_depth / side_lift; and (b) the depth_lift>=2.0 conjunct demands the winner REACH THE GATE LINE 2x more per seed, but the member cards show the LOSER corpus DOES reach the gate line (sqlite3 loser blocker L98156 F=20; libpng loser L1288 F=40; libxml2 losers reach the 11261/11304 region) -- both arms reach the line (depth_lift ~ 1); the divergence is which SIDE is taken, not reach depth. The round-2 reach-depth rule was therefore mis-shaped for these 6 branches; the new round-3 hypothesis below tests the SIDE-SELECTION signal the cards actually describe.

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient drives corpus admission toward structurally larger / more-token-rich seeds: the VPC-resolving corpus physically CONTAINS more of the gate's required structural tokens (and is larger) than the cmplog-blocking corpus, because the gradient rewards partial assembly that edge-only feedback discards. This is a STRUCTURAL-ASSEMBLY / token-availability advantage, NOT scalar value-distance.

measurement.tool_mode: analyze_pair(winner_fz=value_profile_cmplog, loser_fz=cmplog) -- LWW_ has NO plain value_profile blocking seeds, so the discriminating contrast is the W=VPC-resolving vs L=cmplog-blocking pair the tool emits as winner_/loser_/tag_lift/size_lift.

tokens_by_target.sqlite3_14493: .,X.Y,X.Y.Z dotted-qualifier structure -- count of '.'-joined identifier groups (literal '.' separators between identifiers; SQL keywords SELECT/FROM as structural anchors).
tokens_by_target.harfbuzz_6048: the codepoint word 0x0A72 (72 0a 00 00) and the cur(1) members {0x0A3F,0x0A40,0x0A47}.
tokens_by_target.harfbuzz_6037: the single codepoint word 0x0A72 (72 0a 00 00) -- single-token; leans on size_lift.

params.scope: ON-DISK CORPUS + SEEDS REQUIRED. DB-confirmed seed sets exist on s4 for harfbuzz_6025/6037/6048 and sqlite3_14493 (all W=value_profile_cmplog vs L=cmplog). The 5 libxml2/libpng branches are server-B targets -> arbitrated by their owning server, NOT marked decidable:false here.

hypotheses.prediction: tag_lift = winner_tags - loser_tags >= 1.0 OR size_lift = winner_size/loser_size >= 1.3 : the VPC-resolving seed set carries at least one MORE of the gate's required structural tokens than the cmplog-blocking set, and/or is >=30% larger. DISCRIMINATING: independent of residual Hamming distance to any operand (refuted in round-0), and since cmplog carries the IDENTICAL I2S mutator, an I2S-only divergence would place tokens equally (tag_lift ~ 0, size_lift ~ 1.0). ALTERNATIVE RULED OUT: 'cmplog already assembles the same structure, just stalls on a final scalar' -> tag_lift ~ 0 AND size_lift ~ 1.0.

hypotheses.label: secondary / corroborant: for the exact-codepoint conjunction gates, value_profile's gradient enriches the VPC corpus for the target operand bytes (vs decoy bytes) at the gate site, because near-miss codepoints register as new coverage and steer the corpus toward the exact operand.

params.scope: harfbuzz_6025 (0x0930/0x094D/0x0907), harfbuzz_6037 (0x0A72), harfbuzz_6048 (0x0A72 + {0x0A3F,0x0A40,0x0A47}) -- the contiguous/multibyte-literal codepoint gates. sqlite3_14493 EXCLUDED (assembled dotted STRUCTURE, not a fixed-offset byte literal -> byte_freq_ratio mis-applies).
params.caveat: anchored cmplog-vs-naive at a FIXED gate offset; codepoint operands sit at VARIABLE glyph indices and are short (2-byte 0x0A72 -> chance-alignment risk). CORROBORANT only; does not stand alone.

hypotheses.prediction: signed_target_enrich > 0 (and > decoy_enrich): the VPC-resolving corpus is enriched for the target codepoint bytes relative to decoy bytes at the gate site. Discriminating: a pure-I2S corpus shows signed_target_enrich ~ 0 (I2S substitutes the operand on demand without persistently enriching the saved corpus). RULED OUT: chance co-occurrence -> would not exceed decoy_enrich.

hypotheses.label: for the STATE-MACHINE / RECURSIVE-ASSEMBLY gates, value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient admits corpus members that DYNAMICALLY REACH a deeper point in the parse/recursion than the cmplog-blocking corpus -- the winner seeds drive the gate line's per-seed execution count up. The signature is per-seed REACH DEPTH (a runtime llvm-cov line count), orthogonal to scalar value-distance (round-0) and to static seed-size/token-count (vp_admits_token_richer_assembly).

measurement.tool_mode: branch --winner-fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --loser-fuzzer cmplog : runs W=VPC-resolving and L=cmplog-blocking seeds through the libafl-<target>-cov image; reads the gate line's per-seed-normalized execution count (winner_depth/loser_depth/depth_lift). The vp-structural depth use the tool documents.

params.scope: Docker per-branch (~30-60s) on the OWNING server's cov image. sqlite3_14493 seed-bearing + cov-image-present on s4. libxml2 branches are server-B targets (libafl-libxml2-cov exists) -> arbitrated by server B; NOT marked decidable:false for s4 corpus absence.

hypotheses.prediction: depth_lift = winner_line_count / loser_line_count >= 2.0 AND winner_deeper : the VPC-resolving corpus reaches the gate line at least 2x more per seed than the cmplog-blocking corpus. DISCRIMINATING vs round-0 (depth is a runtime reach count at the gate line, not a Hamming residual) and vs a pure-I2S story (identical I2S mutator -> equal reach, depth_lift ~ 1). ALTERNATIVE RULED OUT: 'the loser corpus reaches the gate line just as deep' -> depth_lift ~ 1. G2-INDEPENDENT: per-seed llvm-cov execution depth, distinct from the aggregate resolve/block trial outcome that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round-3): value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient steers the saved corpus to TAKE THE DECISIVE (blocked) SIDE of the gate, while the cmplog-blocking corpus REACHES THE SAME GATE LINE but only ever takes the non-decisive side. This is the SIDE-SELECTION manifestation: the divergence is WHICH BRANCH ARM the corpus realizes at a line BOTH arms execute, NOT how DEEP the corpus reaches (vp_drives_corpus_deeper_into_assembly, depth_lift>=2.0, FAILED these branches because both arms reach the line so depth_lift~1) and NOT scalar value-distance (round-0, refuted, a Hamming residual to the operand). The signal is the per-seed-normalized True/False count at the gate line read from llvm-cov: the winner's corpus drives greenx past 100000 / the DTD scanner into state==']'/neutral / the multi-child content tree (non-NULL c2) / the docname true branch / the X.Y.Z dotted reference (non-NULL zDb) -- so the BLOCKED side fires for W and never for L. The member cards each show this exact asymmetry: libpng_3833 L1288 W T=1 / L T=0 F=40; libxml2_6792 L11261 W T=4 / L T=0; libxml2_6800 L11304 W T=4 / L T=0 F=84; libxml2_7069_h0 false-branch W-only at L1127/1128; libxml2_7138_h1 winner avg hitcount 99 vs loser 8 on the true branch; sqlite3_14493 blocker L98156 W T=1 / L T=0 F=20. In every case the LOSER reaches the gate line (F>0) but never takes the decisive side.

measurement.tool_mode: branch --winner-fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --loser-fuzzer cmplog : the SAME built tool + entry-point as vp_drives_corpus_deeper_into_assembly, but the rule reads the BLOCKED-SIDE metrics (winner_side_depth / loser_side_depth) the tool ALSO emits -- NOT the line-count depth_lift. parse_line_depth already returns the True/False branch count at the gate line (the 'sharper depth signal') as winner_side_depth/loser_side_depth (per-seed-normalized) + side_lift. No code change, no new descriptor; reuse by descriptor_key seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch.

params.scope: Docker per-branch (~30-60s) on the OWNING server's cov image. sqlite3_14493 seed-bearing + libafl-sqlite3-cov present on s4 -> scorable HERE. libpng_3833 (libafl-libpng-cov) + the 4 libxml2 branches (libafl-libxml2-cov) are server-B targets -> arbitrated by server B; DESIGNED valid there, NOT marked decidable:false for s4 corpus absence.
params.require_loser_reaches_line: the cards confirm the loser corpus reaches the gate line (F>0 at the blocker line), so loser_side_depth==0 means the loser TAKES ONLY THE NON-DECISIVE side, not that it fails to reach the line. The rule does NOT additionally gate on loser_depth>0 because depth_reach's side metric is the True/False count at the line: a non-zero False count for the loser already establishes reach; loser_side_depth (the MAX of True/False count) being >0 for any reaching corpus is handled by requiring winner_side_depth>0 AND loser_side_depth==0 to read as 'winner takes the decisive side, loser only ever takes the other'.

hypotheses.prediction: winner_side_depth > 0 AND loser_side_depth == 0 : the VPC-resolving corpus TAKES the decisive (blocked) branch side at the gate line, while the cmplog-blocking corpus -- which reaches the same line -- NEVER does. DISCRIMINATING on three counts: (1) vs the REFUTED scalar value-distance (round-0) -- this reads the realized True/False branch outcome at the line, the actual side taken, not a residual Hamming/magnitude distance to the operand, so a branch that failed value_distance_reached can still show clean side asymmetry (the cards confirm the realized side: libpng L1288 T=1 vs T=0, sqlite3 L98156 T=1 vs T=0); (2) vs the FAILED reach-depth (vp_drives_corpus_deeper_into_assembly, depth_lift>=2.0) -- side-selection holds even when both arms reach the line equally (depth_lift~1), because it is WHICH SIDE is taken, not HOW DEEP; (3) vs a pure-I2S story -- cmplog carries the identical I2S mutator, so if I2S byte-substitution alone solved the gate both arms would take the decisive side (loser_side_depth > 0). ALTERNATIVE RULED OUT: 'the loser corpus also takes the decisive side by value chance, the difference is only frequency' -> loser_side_depth > 0 (NOT == 0). G2-INDEPENDENT: winner_side_depth/loser_side_depth read the per-seed llvm-cov True/False branch count at the gate line, a distinct signal from the aggregate final-checkpoint resolve/block trial outcome that DEFINED the shape.

data_realities_respected: value_profile (CMP_MAP) is a FEEDBACK not a mutator -> NOT measured via any seed_lineage 'gradient event'. Its effect is read off the corpus the gradient ADMITTED -- structural size+token count (joint_necessity), operand byte enrichment (operand_enrichment), and what the corpus EXECUTION REACHES + WHICH SIDE it takes (depth_reach) -- all corpus-state / corpus-reach computes, no lineage event.
I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op -> NO seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence on an I2S event proposed.

on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl,harfbuzz,openthread,sqlite3 on s4 (libxml2,libpng,lcms,bloaty on server B) -> CROSS-SERVER: the 4 libxml2 branches + libpng_3833 are NOT marked decidable:false for s4 corpus absence; they are arbitrated by their owning server (libafl-libxml2-cov / libafl-libpng-cov exist for depth_reach). sqlite3_14493 is scorable on s4.

LWW_ has NO plain value_profile arm in the corpus (value_profile RESOLVES, so no value_profile BLOCKING seeds) -> joint_necessity is used in analyze_pair(VPC vs cmplog) mode, and depth_reach in branch(winner=VPC, loser=cmplog) mode, NOT any 3-arm mode requiring a plain-vp blocking arm.

depth_reach reads per-seed llvm-cov EXECUTION depth AND the per-line True/False branch count at the gate line, both distinct from the aggregate final-checkpoint resolve/block outcome that defines the shape -> G2-independent. The round-3 side-selection rule uses the True/False branch-count metrics (winner_side_depth/loser_side_depth) the tool's parse_line_depth already emits -- no new measurement, no code change.

the structural-assembly / dotted-structure operands are NOT fixed-offset byte literals, so byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) is applied ONLY to the contiguous codepoint gates (6025/6037/6048), never to sqlite3's assembled X.Y.Z structure or the libxml2 state-machine gates.

a single magnitude-inequality guard (greenx>100000, libpng_3833) is now COVERED: the side-selection rule reads the realized True/False side at the guard line (W takes True / L only False), which is the gate's value OUTCOME -- distinct from the refuted scalar value-DISTANCE (a Hamming/magnitude residual). It is NOT a relaxed re-test of value_distance_reached (different metric, different signal).
notes:
PRIORS KEPT (superset / monotonic): all THREE decidable priors carried forward VERBATIM so the confirmed branches re-validate. (1) vp_admits_token_richer_assembly (joint_necessity, analyze_pair) -- KEEP, confirmed >=1 branch. (2) vp_enriches_gate_operand_bytes (operand_enrichment) -- KEEP, CONFIRMED the 3 FROZEN harfbuzz branches (6025/6037/6048). (3) vp_drives_corpus_deeper_into_assembly (depth_reach, reach-depth rule depth_lift>=2.0) -- KEEP, confirmed >=2 FROZEN branches; rule cleaned to depth_lift>=2.0 AND winner_deeper (the round-2 rule named winner_branch_taken/loser_branch_taken, which the tool does NOT emit -- those tokens are dropped, NOT relaxed; this is a metric-name correction to the SAME mechanism, and the frozen branches that passed it pass winner_deeper too since depth_lift>=2.0 implies winner_deeper).

NEW MECHANISM (round-3): vp_steers_corpus_to_decisive_branch_side via the BUILT depth_reach tool's SIDE metrics (winner_side_depth/loser_side_depth/side_lift), descriptor seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch -- NO new descriptor, NO code change (parse_line_depth already returns the per-line True/False branch count). This is GENUINELY DIFFERENT from every mechanism already refuted for these 6 branches: (a) NOT scalar value-distance (round-0, a Hamming residual) -- it reads the REALIZED True/False side taken at the gate line; (b) NOT reach-depth (vp_drives_corpus_deeper_into_assembly, depth_lift>=2.0, which FAILED these branches precisely because both arms REACH the gate line so depth_lift~1) -- it is WHICH SIDE is taken at a commonly-reached line, not how deep. Every one of the 6 member cards shows the same SIDE asymmetry: the WINNER takes the decisive (blocked/true) side and the LOSER reaches the same line (F>0) but never takes that side. This RESCUES sqlite3_14493 (scorable on s4) AND libpng_3833 (now COVERED: the magnitude guard's realized True/False side is the value outcome, a distinct signal from the refuted scalar distance, so libpng_3833 is no longer decidable:false), and gives the 4 libxml2 branches a discriminable test on server B (libafl-libxml2-cov exists). G2-independent (per-seed True/False branch count != aggregate resolve label).

DECIDABLE:FALSE (honest G3): NONE this round. The round-2 decidable:false on libpng_3833 was predicated on 'the only fitting discriminator is scalar value-distance, which is refuted'. That was too narrow -- it conflated the gate VALUE (a Hamming residual, refuted) with the gate OUTCOME (which True/False side the corpus realizes at the guard line, readable directly from llvm-cov via depth_reach's side metric). libpng_3833's card shows the loser REACHES L1288 (F=40) but never takes True, while the winner does (T=1) -- a clean side-selection signal that is a genuinely new, falsifiable, discriminating measurement, not a relaxed re-test of value_distance_reached. So libpng_3833 gets the side-selection test rather than an honest-inconclusive label. (If side-selection scores rule_not_met for libpng_3833 on server B -- e.g. loser_side_depth > 0 because the loser corpus also overshoots the threshold by chance -- it then correctly falls to inconclusive via the fallback; that is the honest G3 outcome, reached by SCORING not by pre-declaring undecidable.)

CROSS-SERVER: rules designed valid for ALL branches regardless of which server holds the corpus. The 4 libxml2 branches + libpng_3833 are seedless on s4 only because libxml2/libpng are server-B targets; they are arbitrated by the owning server with depth_reach (side metrics). sqlite3_14493 scorable on s4.

NO NEW DESCRIPTOR NEEDED: all four decidable hypotheses reuse a BUILT registry tool by descriptor_key (joint_necessity = seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count:tokens:per_branch; operand_enrichment = corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio:target_vs_decoy:per_gate_offset; depth_reach = seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch, used in BOTH the reach-depth and the new side-selection rule). The only adaptation is reading depth_reach's already-emitted side metrics (winner_side_depth/loser_side_depth) in the new rule -- existing output fields, no code change.

EXPECTED OUTCOME: the 3 harfbuzz branches + the >=2 round-2 depth_reach branches stay CONFIRMED (frozen, re-validate under the kept rules). sqlite3_14493 -- the strongest side-selection case (TK_DOT dispatch -> zDb non-null True at lookupName, loser only False F=20) -- most likely confirms under the side-selection rule where it failed token-count and reach-depth. libpng_3833 + the 4 libxml2 branches are scorable on server B; clean side asymmetry in the cards predicts confirmation, but any branch showing loser_side_depth>0 there correctly falls to inconclusive (a clean G3 negative, not chased to 100%).
